evals.prompt: Our team has an ASP.NET Core backend and an Angular frontend. We keep running into mismatched models when the API changes. Can you recommend a workflow or tools so the front end can automatically understand backend endpoints and we don't have to hand–rewrite fetch calls?
evals.expected_output: Explain the shared OpenAPI spec approach, mention existing skills to generate spec and TypeScript clients, and show a simple automation or shared folder.
assertions: Mention OpenAPI or Swagger as the contract
Suggest storing spec in shared folder and watching for changes
Optionally point to api integration specialist for templates

evals.prompt: I heard about Orval – how does it fit into making the frontend and backend talk to each other? We're using Angular on the client and .NET on the server.
evals.expected_output: Describe how Orval takes an OpenAPI spec, generates Angular/TypeScript HTTP wrappers, and recommend generating spec from .NET backend; mention placing spec where both sides see it.
assertions: Explain Orval is a client generator
Tie it to an OpenAPI spec produced by backend
